Title 18-C: PROBATE OF WILLS AND ADMINISTRATION

Chapter 3: PROBATE OF WILLS AND ADMINISTRATION

§3-108. Probate, testacy and appointment proceedings; ultimate time limit

No informal probate or appointment proceeding and no formal testacy or appointment proceeding, other than a proceeding to probate a will previously probated at the testator's domicile and appointment proceedings relating to an estate in which there has been a prior appointment, may be commenced more than 3 years after the decedent's death, except:

1. Will probated. If a decedent is believed to have died without leaving a will and a will is later found, the time limit does not apply to a proceeding to contest the will; and

2. Determination of heirs. A proceeding to determine heirs of an intestate may be commenced at any time after the decedent's death.

The Revisor's Office cannot provide legal advice or interpretation of Maine law to the public. Office of the Revisor of Statutes.
